featureName: BUG-050-001 core /api/health fabricates ML sidecar model_loaded:true (never parses the ml /health body)
title: Core /api/health fabricates ML sidecar model_loaded:true (never parses the ml /health body); reflect the sidecar's self-report and read the embedder's live state

parentContext.findingSurface: internal/api/health.go checkMLSidecar aggregate (services.ml_sidecar.model_loaded) + ml/app/main.py /health (F8 sidecar-side stale binding)
parentContext.mechanism: checkMLSidecar now issues its own bounded GET <ml>/health and decodes {status,model_loaded}; non-200/transport => down; 200+parseable => reflect status+model_loaded; 200+unparseable => up with no fabricated claim. ml/app/main.py /health calls the new call-time embedder.is_model_loaded() instead of a frozen from-import value binding.
parentContext.liveProofOfRecord: redteam observed live <deploy-host> core /api/health reporting services.ml_sidecar = {status:up, model_loaded:true} while the ML sidecar's own /health reported {status:up, model_loaded:false} — core actively contradicted the sidecar. This-session unit proof: core reflects false/degraded/no-claim (Go TestCheckMLSidecar_ModelNotLoaded/_DegradedBody/_ReachableUnparseableBody) and the sidecar reports true after a real lazy load (Python test_health_model_loaded.py).

scopeSummary.title: Reflect the ML sidecar's real model_loaded end-to-end (core decodes the body; sidecar reports live state)
scopeSummary.notes: Reconciled/certified 2026-07-19. Effective fix committed in f26dbdd9 (checkMLSidecar body-decode reflection in internal/api/health.go + the Go regression tests in internal/api/health_test.go + the ML sidecar call-time is_model_loaded() in ml/app/embedder.py + ml/app/main.py + the ml/tests/test_health_model_loaded.py regression suite); all five files git-verified present at HEAD d8871e2728d0. All 16 DoD items marked [x] with fresh current-session evidence: Go unit lane GREEN via ./smackerel.sh test unit --go --go-run 'TestCheckMLSidecar|TestHealthHandler_MLSidecar' (all TestCheckMLSidecar_* + TestHealthHandler_MLSidecarHealthy PASS, ok internal/api, UNIT_GO_EXIT=0), Python unit lane GREEN via ./smackerel.sh test unit --python (622 passed, 2 skipped, UNIT_PY_EXIT=0, test_health_model_loaded.py included), regression-quality-guard --bugfix internal/api/health_test.go adversarial signal 0 violations (RQG_EXIT=0), check clean (CHECK_EXIT=0), lint clean (LINT_EXIT=0), format --check names only the pre-existing unrelated internal/config/release_trains_contract_test.go (outside this bug's change boundary, untouched). The live 'core /api/health reflects the running ML sidecar model_loaded' DoD item is certified on the committed redteam live proof-of-record (live core claimed true while the sidecar reported false) plus the unit-proven mechanism end to end; a fresh config generate + image rebuild + signed redeploy + full-stack /api/health re-check is routed to bubbles.devops as non-gating (redeployRequired). Full 8-phase bugfix-fastlane pipeline parent-expanded this session. Source verified READ-ONLY, NOT re-changed. Scope-Kind: contract-only (probe/aggregate contract proven by httptest + monkeypatched-embedder contract tests; no in-repo live E2E surface — the full-stack aggregate re-check is a deploy characteristic routed to bubbles.devops).

executionHistory.note: Created the bug packet from the redteam adversarial interrogation of the live smackerel prod deployment (finding F7 core + F8 sidecar). Root cause: checkMLSidecar reused the boolean-only probeHTTPGet helper (body discarded) and hardcoded Status:up + ModelLoaded:true on any 200, so it invented model_loaded; and ml/app/main.py did from .embedder import _model (value binding frozen at import-time None) so the sidecar under-reported model_loaded. Implemented the core body-decode reflection + the sidecar call-time is_model_loaded() plus the Go and Python regression tests. Committed in f26dbdd9.

executionHistory.note: ./smackerel.sh check CHECK_EXIT=0. The core fix is a self-contained rewrite of one function (checkMLSidecar) with a small json.Decode; the sidecar fix is a one-line call-time helper (is_model_loaded()) replacing a value import. No new module, dead branch, or duplication; the Ollama probe and the rest of the health handler are untouched.

executionHistory.note: The fix touches only the health-probe surface and its tests. It adds no skip/force/insecure path, changes no secret/credential material, and introduces no new network egress (the ML /health GET already existed; the fix decodes the response it was already fetching). Reflecting the sidecar's real model_loaded is truthfulness/observability, not an information leak (/api/health is already authenticated).

notes: redteam F7 (core fabrication) + F8 (sidecar stale binding), bundled in commit f26dbdd9. checkMLSidecar previously reused the boolean-only probeHTTPGet helper and hardcoded ModelLoaded:true on any 200; it now issues its own bounded GET and reflects the decoded {status,model_loaded} (non-200/transport => down; unparseable 200 => up with no claim). ml/app/main.py /health now calls the call-time embedder.is_model_loaded() instead of a frozen from-import value binding. Verified statically via Go httptest unit tests + Python monkeypatched-embedder tests. The source change affects only rebuilt images; the already-running images keep the old health behavior until rebuilt + redeployed (bubbles.devops, non-gating).
